question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted TokenAirdropRequest with a manipulated limit value at MAX_LIMIT to force the service to return airdrop records whose aggregated fee amounts exceed the design-intended per-request fee budget, causing fee accounting to diverge from expected parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user call getAirdrops() with an accountId that resolves to a known high-volume airdrop sender, causing the service to return PENDING airdrop records with inflated fee amounts that misrepresent the actual charged_tx_fee stored in the database, leading to incorrect fee display outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of fee-field validation in getAirdrops() to retrieve TokenAirdrop records where the amount field contains values that bypass the expected fee schedule constraints, allowing the attacker to observe or infer fee modifications outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TokenAirdropRequest with AirdropRequestType.OUTSTANDING and AirdropRequestType.PENDING simultaneously by manipulating the type field to cause the underlying jOOQ query to use the wrong baseField (SENDER_ACCOUNT_ID vs RECEIVER_ACCOUNT_ID), returning airdrop records with fee amounts that do not correspond to the correct transaction context?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a tokenIds Bound with a GT operator set to Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 to trigger integer overflow in getAdjustedLowerRangeValue(), causing the query to return airdrop records with fee amounts outside the intended range, effectively bypassing fee parameter constraints?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a serialNumbers Bound with operator LT and value 1 to cause adjustUpperBound() to return 0 (via lowerBound--), making the query silently return zero results and masking pending airdrop fee records that should be visible, causing fee data to appear modified outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getBounds() priority logic in TokenAirdropRequest where entityIds takes precedence over tokenIds, by supplying both entityIds and tokenIds bounds simultaneously, to cause the secondary bound to be silently dropped from the query, returning airdrop records with fee amounts that bypass the intended token-level fee filter?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with entityIds Bound containing two EQ operators for the same value to cause verifySingleOccurrence() to throw an IllegalArgumentException that leaks internal query structure details, enabling the attacker to infer fee-related field names and craft subsequent requests that modify fee visibility outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a tokenIds Bound with both an EQ operator and a GT operator simultaneously to trigger the verifyEqualOrRange() exception path, causing an error response that reveals whether specific token IDs with custom fee schedules exist in the database, enabling fee schedule enumeration outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the adjustLowerRange() method in Bound by providing a range where adjustedLower equals adjustedUpper (hasEqualBounds() returns true) to rewrite the lower bound to EQ, causing the jOOQ query to use an equality condition instead of a range condition on TOKEN_ID, potentially returning airdrop records with fee amounts that should have been excluded by the range filter?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a serialNumbers Bound with a GT operator and value 0 to cause getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() to return 1, bypassing serial number 0 (which maps to null via mapToNullIfZero in TokenAirdropMapper), thereby hiding fungible token airdrop fee records that use serial number 0 as a sentinel value, causing fee data to appear incomplete outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mapToNullIfZero() function in TokenAirdropMapper to cause fungible token airdrops (which have serialNumber=0) to appear as NFT-type airdrops with null serial numbers in the response, causing downstream fee calculation logic to apply incorrect fee schedules (royalty vs fractional) outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit value of 1 combined with a DESC sort order to retrieve only the most recent airdrop record, and by iterating through all accounts, enumerate airdrop fee amounts one record at a time to reconstruct the full fee schedule of a token outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getSort() method in TokenAirdropsController where lastSerial == DEFAULT_SERIAL_VALUE (0L) causes the sort to omit SERIAL_NUMBER, generating a next-page link that skips serial-number-based pagination, causing the attacker to receive duplicate airdrop fee records across pages and artificially inflate observed fee totals outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where entityIds Bound has hasLowerAndUpper() true but adjustedLower > adjustedUpper for a non-primary sort field (primarySortField=false), bypassing the IllegalArgumentException guard in the Bound constructor, and causing the jOOQ query to execute with an inverted range condition that returns airdrop records with fee amounts outside the intended filter window?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a tokenIds Bound with operator LTE and value Long.MAX_VALUE to cause adjustUpperBound() to return Long.MAX_VALUE unchanged, effectively removing the upper bound on token ID filtering and returning all airdrop records regardless of token, exposing fee amounts for tokens the attacker should not be able to enumerate, outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SORT_ORDERS map in TokenAirdropRepositoryCustomImpl by providing an AirdropRequestType not present in the map (causing getOrDefault to return an empty map), resulting in a null order list passed to jOOQ's orderBy(), which may cause the database to return records in an undefined order that exposes fee amounts in an unintended sequence outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an accountId that resolves via entityService.lookup() to EntityId.EMPTY (e.g., by supplying a non-existent account alias), causing the getBaseCondition() to use id=0 as the WHERE clause value, which may match system accounts with special fee schedules and return their airdrop fee records outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AirdropState.PENDING filter in findAll() by observing that only PENDING state records are returned, and use the absence of CLAIMED/CANCELLED records to infer which airdrop transactions had their fees charged (CLAIMED) vs refunded (CANCELLED), effectively reconstructing fee modification history outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a serialNumbers Bound with operator GT and value Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 to trigger a long overflow in getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() (lowerBound++ wraps to Long.MIN_VALUE), causing the jOOQ query to use a negative serial number lower bound that matches all NFT airdrop records and exposes their fee amounts outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getInclusiveRangeValue() method in Bound by providing a LT operator with value Long.MIN_VALUE to cause value -= 1L to overflow to Long.MAX_VALUE, generating an inclusive range value that wraps around and matches all possible token IDs, returning airdrop records with fee amounts across all tokens outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user provide two EntityIdRangeParameter entries both with GT operators to the receiverIds parameter, causing verifySingleOccurrence() to throw an exception that reveals the internal parameter name (RECEIVER_ID constant), enabling the attacker to map the API's internal field structure and craft fee-targeted queries outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the adjustUpperRange() method in Bound where if lower.operator() == EQ, upper is set to lower, causing a secondary bound to be rewritten to an equality condition, and by chaining this with the getBounds() priority logic, force the query to use an unintended equality filter on TOKEN_ID that returns airdrop records with specific fee amounts outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a @Size(max=2) array with exactly 2 EntityIdRangeParameter entries where both have LTE operators, bypassing verifySingleOccurrence() (which only checks GT/GTE together), causing the Bound to have two upper bounds where only the last one processed is stored, silently discarding the first upper bound and widening the fee record filter outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the linkFactory.create() pagination mechanism by manipulating the EXTRACTOR function's output (which uses tokenAirdrop.getSerialNumber() with a null check) to generate a next-page link with serialNumber=0L that, when followed, returns a different set of airdrop fee records than expected, causing fee data inconsistency across pages outside design parameters?
